text.title: WebSocket Server Patterns
text.summary: ConnectionManager with channel subscriptions, per-connection heartbeat, and Redis Pub/Sub scale-out.

rule.statement: Define a versioned message envelope: {v, type, channel, seq, ts, payload}. Bump v for breaking protocol changes. Clients deduplicate by seq across reconnects.
rule.rationale: Without a versioned envelope, protocol changes cannot be rolled out gradually. Without seq, clients cannot detect duplicate delivery after reconnect.

rule.statement: Implement server-side ping/pong: server disconnects a client after 2 missed pong responses. Client sends ping every 25–30 seconds.
rule.rationale: Without heartbeats, stale connections accumulate silently. NAT gateways and load balancers close idle TCP connections without RST, leaving the server with zombie sockets.

rule.statement: Authenticate WebSocket connections at the HTTP upgrade stage using a short-lived ticket obtained from a REST endpoint, or via a signed cookie. Never put JWT tokens in query strings.
rule.rationale: Query strings appear in server access logs; JWT tokens in logs are a credential leak. Cookies with HttpOnly/Secure are not accessible to JavaScript XSS.

rule.statement: For horizontal scaling, broadcast messages via Redis Pub/Sub instead of local fan-out. Each server node subscribes to channels and delivers to its locally connected clients.
rule.rationale: Connections are pinned to one process. Without a shared pubsub bus, a message published to node A never reaches clients connected to node B.

example.code:
import redis.asyncio as redis import asyncio, json
class DistributedConnectionManager: def __init__(self, redis_url: str = "redis://localhost:6379"): self.active_connections: dict = {} self.channel_subscriptions: dict = {} self.redis_url = redis_url
async def start(self): self.redis = await redis.from_url(self.redis_url) self.pubsub = self.redis.pubsub() asyncio.create_task(self._listen())
async def _listen(self): await self.pubsub.psubscribe("ws:channel:*") async for message in self.pubsub.listen(): if message["type"] != "pmessage": continue channel = message["channel"].decode().removeprefix("ws:channel:") data = json.loads(message["data"]) await self._local_broadcast(channel, data)
async def broadcast(self, channel: str, message: dict): await self.redis.publish(f"ws:channel:{channel}", json.dumps(message))
